title: BUG: Add delimiters to NameObject.renumber_table
body:
Per the spec, name objects cannot contain unescaped delimiters, which include angle, curly and square brackets (currently missing).

I've attached a sample input PDF with a valid, correctly escaped name object. When appending this to a new PdfWriter the output is invalid (incorrectly escaped):

```
>>> w = PdfWriter()
>>> w.append('nameobj.pdf')
>>> w.write('out.pdf')
```
[out.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20173760/out.pdf)
[nameobj.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20173761/nameobj.pdf)

step_plan.summary: Fix PDF NameObject serialization by escaping all delimiter characters (including angle, square, and curly brackets) when writing, preventing PdfWriter from emitting invalid name syntax after append+write. Add regression tests to verify both `renumber()`-based serialization and end-to-end writer round-trips preserve correctly escaped names.

steps.operation: Extend NameObject escaping to include missing PDF delimiter characters by adding `<`, `>`, `[`, `]`, `{`, `}` entries to the class-level `renumber_table` (as `#3C/#3E/#5B/#5D/#7B/#7D`) and ensure the method continues to apply the table to raw name bytes.
steps.reason: PDF name objects must not contain unescaped delimiter characters; current write-side escaping omits angle/square/curly brackets, causing valid input names to be re-emitted invalidly after append+write. The escaping table is consumed by `renumber()`, so this is the central place to align serialization with the delimiter set used during parsing.
steps.side_effects: Changes serialized output bytes for any name containing these characters; may affect downstream tests that assert exact output, and could slightly change round-trip behavior for previously (incorrectly) written PDFs.

steps.operation: Add/adjust a regression test that exercises the writer path: create a NameObject containing delimiter characters (e.g., `A<B>[C]{D}`), place it into an object written by `PdfWriter` (as a dictionary value and/or key), write to bytes, then re-read and assert the name is preserved and/or the raw output contains the expected `#XX` escapes.
steps.reason: The reported failure happens through `PdfWriter.append()` + `write()` (serialization), not just `renumber()` in isolation. A round-trip test prevents regressions by validating that writer output remains spec-compliant and readable when names include delimiter characters.
steps.side_effects: May require importing writer/reader utilities and using in-memory streams; could increase test runtime slightly and may need updates if test suite conventions differ (e.g., existing helpers/fixtures).

steps.operation: Ensure parsing/writing consistency by adding a focused test that confirms a name containing escaped delimiters is correctly parsed by `read_from_stream` (e.g., input bytes `/A#3CB#5B#5D#7B#7D`) and results in the expected `NameObject` value, matching what `write_to_stream` produces.
steps.reason: `delimiter_pattern` already treats `< > [ ] { }` as delimiters during parsing; this test ensures the read path correctly interprets hex-escaped delimiter sequences and stays consistent with the updated write-side escaping used during append+write flows.
steps.side_effects: No behavioral change intended if parsing already works; adds coverage that may expose existing edge cases in name decoding and could require small adjustments if current decoding differs from the spec expectations.
